commit.message:
fix: preserve utf-8 boundaries in detector heads

Keep bounded detector head reads while decoding only the valid UTF-8 prefix when truncation lands inside a multibyte sequence, avoiding false negatives for otherwise valid files.

Add regression tests for exact multibyte boundaries and partial multibyte suffixes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement ID-based short share links

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix stale page on reload: switch index.html to network-first SW strategy

The service worker was using cache-first for index.html, meaning any
update to the page code was not visible until the new SW installed and
activated — which required all tabs to close first. This caused
intermittent missing appName/icon rendering (and other stale-code issues)
because the old cached page was served while fresh API data was returned.

Changes:
- index.html / '/' now use network-first (network failure falls back to cache)
- Truly static assets (manifest, favicon, qrcode.min.js) keep cache-first
- API and all other requests bypass the SW cache entirely
- Added skipWaiting() + clients.claim() so new SW activates immediately
  without waiting for all tabs to close
- Bumped cache name to web-notifications-v5
- Updated copilot-instructions.md to reflect new strategy

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: ring color changes with progress zone [task-5.2]

and calorie-viz spec scenarios.

commit.message:
fix: perf 缓存补全 DETAILED_ACCESS_REPORT 和 REPEAT_CYCLE

perf_cache 原先只缓存 COMPUTE_REPORT.csv，缓存命中时不恢复
DETAILED_ACCESS_REPORT.csv 和 REPEAT_CYCLE.csv，导致后续
power pipeline 因找不到这两个文件而失败。

现在 _save/_try_load_perf_cache 同时处理三个产物。
已清除旧的不完整缓存。

commit.message:
Install Vercel Speed Insights (#5)

* Install Vercel Speed Insights

Successfully installed and configured Vercel Speed Insights for this Next.js project.

## Changes Made

### 1. Package Installation
- Installed `@vercel/speed-insights` version 2.0.0 using pnpm
- Updated package.json dependencies to include the new package

### 2. Code Integration
Modified: `src/app/layout.tsx`
- Added import statement: `import { SpeedInsights } from '@vercel/speed-insights/next'`
- Integrated the `<SpeedInsights />` component in the root layout's body element
- Placed component alongside the existing `<Analytics />` component for consistency

### 3. Implementation Details
- Followed the official Vercel documentation for Next.js App Router (v13.5+)
- Used framework-specific import from '@vercel/speed-insights/next' for optimal integration
- Component is added unconditionally (not gated by NODE_ENV) as per latest documentation
- Preserved existing code structure and formatting

### 4. Verification Steps Completed
✓ Linter check passed (biome lint) - No issues found
✓ Build verification passed (next build) - Production build successful
✓ Lock file updated (pnpm-lock.yaml) - All dependencies resolved correctly
✓ No test failures (no tests defined in this project)

### Files Modified
- package.json - Added @vercel/speed-insights dependency
- pnpm-lock.yaml - Updated lock file with new dependency tree
- src/app/layout.tsx - Integrated SpeedInsights component

## Next Steps
After deployment to Vercel:
1. Enable Speed Insights in the Vercel dashboard for this project
2. Monitor Core Web Vitals data after user visits
3. Data will be available in the Vercel Speed Insights dashboard

## Notes
- The SpeedInsights component automatically handles production vs development environment detection
- No additional configuration is required for basic usage
- The component works alongside the existing Vercel Analytics integration

commit.message:
Throttle event log and stats via requestAnimationFrame

Root cause of stale book: addLog did synchronous DOM manipulation on every
message, starving requestAnimationFrame callbacks for book rendering.

Fix:
- addLog: buffer entries in memory, flush to DOM in batch via rAF
- updateStats: coalesce via rAF instead of updating DOM every message
- Add Event Log toggle checkbox (Enabled/Disabled) to stop DOM work entirely
- Reduce MAX_LOG from 500 to 200

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: load daglog from localStorage on startup [task-4.2]

On setup(), loadLog() reads today's key (cm_log_YYYY-MM-DD) from
localStorage and parses the stored JSON, or starts with an empty
array when no entry exists for today. This makes log data survive
page refreshes and is automatically scoped to the current day.

commit.message:
fix: remove @secure() from appInsightsConnectionString Bicep output

Azure ARM omits the value field from secure outputs in the deployment
API response. The deploy.ps1 script accesses \.appInsightsConnectionString.value
after parsing the deployment JSON, causing a PropertyNotFoundStrict error
under Set-StrictMode -Version Latest.

Application Insights connection strings are not true secrets — they are
routinely embedded in app configs and client-side telemetry code — so
@secure() is not appropriate here.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: persist daglog to localStorage on every change [task-4.1]

Added todayKey() helper (returns cm_log_YYYY-MM-DD) and saveLog()
which serialises dagLog to localStorage. saveLog() is called from
addToLog() and removeFromLog() so every mutation is immediately
persisted, surviving a page refresh.

commit.message:
fix: CACHE_HIT 状态被误判为失败导致 pipeline 报错退出

perf pipeline 的 ok_count 只计 'OK'，忽略了 'CACHE_HIT'，
导致缓存命中时 ok_count=0 → exit(1) → pipeline.py RuntimeError。
power pipeline 同步修复以防后续加缓存时出同样问题。

automatically because drawDagLog reads the live array.
The kcal text is shifted left to make room for the hit area.
Hover turns the icon terracotta-red for clear affordance.

* Stage 2: UX Workflow & Wiring Spec + BUILD_PROGRESS_TRACKER update (D1-D4)

Produce MMM Stage 2 UX Workflow & Wiring Spec covering:
- 17 user journeys (J-01 through J-17) with complete screen interactions

Maintain bounded trade history per instrument (last 50)

recent trade history is sent as Trade events after the MBO book snapshot.
Trades are always recorded (even without subscribers) so late subscribers
get immediate history.

- Add comprehensive logging to storage service for debugging persistence
- Improve cache loading logic in hooks (get all data before modifying)

- Update storage tests to verify logging behavior

feat: live food search filter in sketch.js [task-2.1]

Implemented filterFoods() that case-insensitively filters the foods
array on naam, returns empty array for queries under 2 chars, capped
at 5 results. Search input uses p5.dom createInput() positioned
relative to canvas. draw() is driven by noLoop/redraw so filtering
only happens in the input handler, not every frame.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

MBO snapshot: send individual orders instead of aggregated MBP levels

Root cause: MBP snapshot + MBO incremental events = inconsistent state.
Pre-snapshot OrderDeleted events couldn't update the book because individual
orders weren't tracked. This caused stale levels and a crossed book display.

Fix: on subscribe, send BookSnapshot(0,0) as reset marker followed by
individual OrderAdded events for every order in the book. The frontend now
has complete MBO state from the start, so all subsequent OrderAdded/Updated/
Deleted events are correctly applied.

Frontend changes:
- formatPrice: show full decimal precision (was capping at 4, hiding price
  differences between levels)
- Throttle book renders via requestAnimationFrame to handle MBO snapshot
  burst without DOM thrashing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: stream file primitive hashing

Replace full-file reads in models hashing paths with buffered streaming SHA-256 so file primitive collection and file-backed content digests keep memory bounded while preserving identical hashes.

Add tests for large multi-chunk files and for file-backed content_digest fallback behavior.

commit.message:
feat: add MCP per-tool authorization filters (Phase 3) (#81)

Implements ToolAuthorizationFilter (tools/call) and ToolListAuthorizationFilter
(tools/list) that enforce per-tool authorization policies. When Authentication.Enabled
is false the filters are never registered — zero behaviour change for existing
deployments.

- AuthorizationHelpers: HasRequiredScopes, HasRequiredRoles, GetToolOverride
- ToolAuthorizationFilter: blocks calls to tools based on FunctionOverride / default
  policy; returns MCP error result with 'Authentication required' or 'Insufficient
  permissions' messages
- ToolListAuthorizationFilter: strips tools the caller cannot invoke from tools/list
  responses
- Program.cs: wires both filters via WithRequestFilters when auth is enabled

commit.message:
Fix timeline empty: add timeout + background refresh for dashboard API calls

Root cause: Dashboard API calls go through the shared rate limiter semaphore.
When agents saturate the semaphore, the dashboard's 15s timeout fires and
returns empty data (no cache on fresh start).

Fix:
- DashboardDataService: 8s timeout on API calls with OperationCanceledException
  handling that returns cached data + schedules a background refresh
- Background refresh fires after 15s delay (when semaphore contention clears)
- Timeline page: auto-retries every 15s when empty (not just rate-limited)
- Increased timeline page timeout to 20s to accommodate the new flow

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: migrate Jira connector from deprecated API v2 to v3

Atlassian removed /rest/api/2/search (CHANGE-2046). The jira Python
library 3.8.0 still calls the old endpoint, causing:
  errorMessages: ['The requested API has been removed. Please migrate
  to the /rest/api/3/search/jql API.']

Changes:
- Added _search_issues_v3() — calls /rest/api/3/search/jql via requests
  directly, bypassing the jira library's hardcoded v2 URL
- fetch_items() now uses _search_issues_v3() instead of jira.search_issues()
- fetch_items() parses raw dict response (v3 returns dicts, not objects)
- Added errorMessages guard: raises JIRAError when Jira returns app-level
  errors in an HTTP 200 response body
- Added deprecation case to _format_jira_error() for the CHANGE-2046 error
- Added requests import (already in requirements.txt)

Tests added (4 new, 10 total in test_jira_error_handling.py):
- test_fetch_items_handles_api_error_gracefully (updated to use requests mock)
- test_fetch_items_uses_v3_api_endpoint (asserts /rest/api/3/search/jql)
- test_fetch_items_raises_on_jira_error_messages_in_200_response
- test_fetch_items_parses_v3_issue_structure (cycle_time_days = 3 days)

Total: 205/205 tests passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace visual tree walk with Style-based font theming

Instead of walking the visual tree to set FontFamily on each TextBlock,
use Style resources ({ThemeResource CmdPal.PrimaryTextStyle} etc.) that
contain both FontFamily and FontSize setters. When the theme changes,
ThemeEngine creates new Style objects in the font override dictionary's
ThemeDictionaries, and a RequestedTheme toggle forces WinUI to
re-resolve the {ThemeResource} bindings.

This approach:
- Eliminates O(n) visual tree walks on every theme change
- Uses standard WinUI resource resolution instead of imperative code
- Is the same pattern CmdPal will use in production (no Tag attrs needed)
- Style objects are re-resolved by {ThemeResource} unlike FontFamily

Changes:
- PreviewStyles.xaml: Add Style resources for each font slot
- CmdPalPreview/DockPreview/ContextMenuPreview.xaml: Replace individual
  FontFamily+FontSize attrs with Style='{ThemeResource CmdPal.*TextStyle}'
- ThemeEngine.cs: Create Style objects instead of bare FontFamily objects
- ThemeDesignerPage.xaml.cs: Replace tree walk with RequestedTheme toggle

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  - Suggests checking Jira URL, server configuration, or network/proxy issues

- test_jira_500_server_error_returns_clear_message()

This addresses the user error: 'Jira connection error: Expecting value: line 2 column 2'
Users will now see: 'Jira API returned invalid data. Check your Jira URL or network connection.'

Total: 202/202 tests passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: bound detector head reads

Use a shared bounded streaming helper for detector head reads so container, MCP, and custom-rule detectors never allocate full files just to inspect the first 8 KiB.

Add tests that verify truncation and that bounded reads stop once the limit is reached, even before a stream reaches EOF.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix AOT compilation: source-gen JSON for all serialization

- MulticastFeedConfig: replace reflection-based JsonSerializer.Deserialize<T>
  with source-generated FeedConfigJsonContext. Replace non-generic
  JsonStringEnumConverter with UseStringEnumConverter in source-gen options.
- Server: enable EnableRequestDelegateGenerator for AOT-compatible MapGet
  delegate binding (eliminates IL2026/IL3050 trim warnings).
- Zero AOT analysis warnings on publish.

feat: expand reader group to all project resources

Reader group now gets read-only access across every resource
provisioned by Terraform, not just the AKS cluster:

- Subscription: Reader (ARM control-plane visibility on all resources)
- AKS cluster: Azure Kubernetes Service RBAC Reader (k8s data-plane)
- ACR: AcrPull (pull images, no push)
- Key Vault: Key Vault Reader (metadata only, no secret values)

Admin group assignments are unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
benchmark: add v0.10.8 three-arm baseline results (Arm A + Arm B)

Arm A (standard prompt): avg 45.50 (Opus 46, Sonnet 44, GPT-5.4 46, Codex 46)
Arm B (terse overlay): avg 45.00 (Opus 46, Sonnet 44, GPT-5.4 45, Codex 45)

Key findings:
- Terse overlay has slight negative effect (-0.50 avg), rejecting brevity-only hypothesis
- MFA Clarity is the most sensitive dimension to terse overlay (GPT models drop 9→8)
- Opus is immune to terse overlay; Sonnet is consistently lowest scorer
- Continuation logic unanimously identified as highest-risk MFA component

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
